title: Chile Constitutional Referendum 2020: Public opinion trajectory regarding the replacement of the 1980 Constitution

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Interpolated trend from Cadem and Pulso Ciudadano
Estimated: Linear progression toward final result

events.description: Official start of the electoral campaign period for the plebiscite.
Intensification of televised political advertising (franja electoral) begins.
First anniversary of the 2019 social unrest (Estallido Social) approaches, mobilizing pro-change sentiment.

agents.name: Sebastian Piñera
agents.description: President of Chile, officially neutral but associated with the status quo.

agents.name: Gabriel Boric
agents.description: Prominent opposition leader and advocate for the new constitution.

agents.name: Chile Vamos
agents.description: Right-wing political coalition, largely favoring the existing constitution.

agents.name: Social Movements
agents.description: Grassroots organizations driving the demand for constitutional change.

agents.name: Catholic Church
agents.description: Neutral institutional actor with moderate social influence.

agents.name: Business Associations
agents.description: Industry groups concerned about economic stability under a new framework.

notes: Pro-side (Apruebo) refers to voters supporting the creation of a new constitution. Against-side (Rechazo) refers to voters supporting the retention of the 1980 constitution. Data quality is high due to consistent polling by Cadem and definitive official results from Servel.
